question: Does the output contain exactly three images: a full-body illustration, a close-up detail shot, and a dynamic combat sketch?
Does the first generated image depict a female character in dark purple fitted combat attire holding two semi-transparent jade short blades?
Does the character in the first generated image exhibit a cold gaze?
Is the second generated image a close-up detail shot focusing on the back of the character's clothing?
Does the second generated image clearly display patterns resembling ancient Chinese 'taotie' motifs (symmetrical beast-face masks or stylized bronze patterns)?
Does the third generated image depict the character in a dynamic leaping attack pose with a sketch-like or action-oriented style?
Does the generated text include detailed lore descriptions and specific skill introductions for the character?
Does the text establish the character within a Chinese-style fantasy setting (e.g., using culturally appropriate names and terminology)?
Is the character's visual identity (hair, face, costume design) consistent between the full-body illustration (Image 1) and the dynamic combat sketch (Image 3)?
Does the fabric color and texture in the close-up detail shot (Image 2) match the dark purple attire shown in the full-body illustration (Image 1)?
Does the text accompanying the first image accurately describe the visual attributes shown (attire, blades, gaze)?
Does the text accompanying the second image explicitly explain the lore or significance of the 'taotie' patterns shown?
Does the text accompanying the third image describe combat skills or mechanics that correspond to the dynamic leaping pose shown?
Are the images placed immediately adjacent to their relevant text sections (e.g., combat sketch next to skill introductions) rather than grouped at the end?
Does the content follow the requested logical progression: full-body overview -> detail close-up -> dynamic action?
Are the generated images free from significant AI distortions or artifacts (e.g., malformed hands, incoherent patterns)?
Do the three images collectively provide non-redundant information (silhouette, texture detail, movement) useful for character design?
